name: Austin City Limits Music Festival
description: Austin City Limits Music Festival (ACL) is a major American music festival held across two consecutive three-day weekends in October in Zilker Park, Austin, Texas. Named after the legendary PBS television series Austin City Limits, the festival celebrates Austin's reputation as the 'Live Music Capital of the World.' ACL features over 130 acts across eight stages, spanning rock, indie, hip-hop, country, folk, electronic, and Latin music. The festival's downtown park setting — with the Austin skyline visible from the grounds — and warm October weather make it one of the most pleasant major festival experiences. Past headliners include The Cure, Radiohead, Pearl Jam, Billie Eilish, and Kendrick Lamar.

name: Bayreuth Festival
description: The Bayreuth Festival is the world's preeminent opera festival, dedicated exclusively to the works of Richard Wagner. Held annually in the Bayreuth Festspielhaus — a theater Wagner himself designed and built on the Green Hill above Bayreuth, Germany — the festival has been running since 1876. The Festspielhaus's unique covered orchestra pit creates the distinctive 'Bayreuth sound' that makes Wagner's operas resonate unlike anywhere else in the world. Tickets are among the most sought-after in classical music, with wait times of up to 10 years. The festival is managed by the Wagner family to this day and remains the definitive venue for experiencing Wagner's Ring cycle and other major works.

name: Beale Street Music Festival
description: Beale Street Music Festival is a three-day celebration of music held in Tom Lee Park on the banks of the Mississippi River in Memphis, Tennessee. As the opening event of Memphis in May, the festival honors the city's deep musical heritage — blues, soul, rock, hip-hop, and gospel all share the stage. Drawing 100,000+ attendees, it features both legendary artists and rising stars. Past performers include Stevie Wonder, Jack White, and Wu-Tang Clan. The festival's Memphis setting is inseparable from its identity — you can feel the history of Beale Street, Sun Studio, and Stax Records in every performance.

name: Bonnaroo Music Festival
description: Bonnaroo Music and Arts Festival is a multi-day camping festival held on a 650-acre farm in Manchester, Tennessee. Since its founding in 2002, Bonnaroo has earned a reputation as one of America's most beloved festival experiences, known for its welcoming community, diverse musical programming, and the unique camaraderie of its campground culture. The lineup spans rock, jam bands, hip-hop, electronic, country, folk, and comedy, with past headliners including Radiohead, Paul McCartney, Stevie Wonder, and Eminem. Bonnaroo's signature What Stage is one of the largest permanent festival stages in North America. The festival emphasizes sustainability, operating on solar and biofuel power and maintaining comprehensive recycling programs.

name: Boston Calling Music Festival
description: Boston Calling Music Festival is a three-day festival held at the Harvard Athletic Complex in Allston, Massachusetts. Since 2013, it has grown into New England's largest music festival, drawing 40,000+ fans per day with a lineup that mixes rock, indie, hip-hop, and electronic acts. The urban setting — on Harvard's campus just minutes from downtown Boston — sets it apart from the rural camping festival model. Past headliners include Tool, Foo Fighters, The Strokes, and Rage Against the Machine. Boston Calling also features a dedicated comedy stage and large-scale art installations.

name: Burning Man
description: Burning Man is a week-long annual gathering held in the Black Rock Desert of northwestern Nevada. Founded in 1986 on Baker Beach in San Francisco before moving to the desert in 1990, it has grown into one of the world's most iconic cultural events. Approximately 80,000 participants create Black Rock City, a temporary metropolis dedicated to art, self-expression, and community. The event culminates in the burning of a large wooden effigy ("the Man") and operates on 10 guiding principles including radical inclusion, gifting, decommodification, and radical self-reliance. While not a traditional music festival, Burning Man features hundreds of sound camps and art cars playing electronic, house, techno, and experimental music around the clock.

name: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
description: The Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival is one of the most iconic and influential music festivals in the world. Held annually at the Empire Polo Club in Indio, California, it spans two consecutive three-day weekends in April. Known for its eclectic lineup that crosses genres from indie rock to hip-hop to electronic dance music, Coachella has become as much a cultural event as a musical one, attracting celebrities, fashion influencers, and art enthusiasts alongside music fans. The festival grounds feature large-scale art installations, immersive sculptures, and curated experiences that transform the desert landscape into a temporary city of creativity. Since its debut in 1999, Coachella has hosted legendary performances — from Radiohead and Daft Punk to Beyoncé's groundbreaking 2018 headlining set, widely regarded as one of the greatest festival performances of all time.

name: Country Thunder
location: Flagship four-day event in Twin Lakes, Wisconsin
city: Flagship four-day event in Twin Lakes
description: Country Thunder is North America's largest multi-location country music festival series, tracing its roots to a 1993 Arizona event originally called Country Jam that was rebranded Country Thunder in 1996. The flagship Wisconsin edition launched that same year at a sprawling 400-acre site in Twin Lakes and now anchors the brand's summer calendar each July, drawing massive camping crowds from Chicago and Milwaukee. The company has since expanded to Florence, Arizona (April), Craven, Saskatchewan (July), Calgary, Alberta (August), and Kissimmee, Florida (November), running on a four-day format with primary and secondary stages. Country Thunder is known less for a single signature venue than for its immersive campground culture — themed RV villages, all-day tailgating, and a fiercely loyal returning fanbase. Past Wisconsin headliners include Luke Bryan, Eric Church, Jason Aldean, Miranda Lambert, Tim McGraw, Morgan Wallen, and Zac Brown Band.

name: Creamfields
description: Creamfields is one of the UK's biggest and longest-running dance music festivals, held in Daresbury, Cheshire over the August bank holiday weekend. Created by the legendary Cream nightclub brand from Liverpool, the festival has been a cornerstone of British electronic music culture since 1998. Creamfields features a massive lineup of EDM, house, techno, trance, and drum and bass across multiple stages, including its iconic Steel Yard super-structure. The festival has expanded internationally with editions in multiple countries. Past performers include Calvin Harris, Tiësto, Deadmau5, and Avicii.

name: Download Festival
description: Download Festival is the UK's premier heavy music festival, held at the spiritual home of rock — Donington Park in Leicestershire. Evolving from the legendary Monsters of Rock festival, Download launched in 2003 and has since become the definitive destination for metal, rock, and punk fans worldwide. The festival draws over 100,000 attendees across three days with a lineup that spans classic metal, hardcore, punk rock, and alternative. Iconic headliners have included Iron Maiden, Black Sabbath, Slipknot, and AC/DC. The Main Stage at Donington is hallowed ground — every major rock and metal band aspires to play it.

name: EDC Las Vegas
description: Electric Daisy Carnival (EDC) Las Vegas is one of the largest electronic dance music festivals in the world, held annually at the Las Vegas Motor Speedway. Produced by Insomniac Events since 1997, EDC has grown from a Los Angeles warehouse party into a three-night spectacle under the desert stars drawing 170,000 fans per night. The festival is renowned for its massive stage productions — kinetic sculptures, pyrotechnics, fireworks, and LED installations that transform the speedway into a neon wonderland. EDC covers every corner of electronic music: house, techno, trance, dubstep, drum and bass, and hardstyle across eight themed stages. The culture of PLUR (Peace, Love, Unity, Respect) runs deep, and EDC is as much about the community as the music.

name: Electric Forest Festival
description: Electric Forest is a music and arts festival held in the Sherwood Forest at Double JJ Resort in Rothbury, Michigan. The festival is defined by its extraordinary setting — a forest illuminated with custom lighting installations, interactive art, and projection-mapped trees that create a surreal, fairy-tale atmosphere after dark. The lineup focuses on electronic music, jam bands, and indie, with Cheese (The String Cheese Incident) as the festival's founding resident act. Electric Forest's devoted community — known as 'Forest Family' — values inclusivity, creativity, and connection, making it one of the most unique and beloved festival cultures in America.

name: Electric Picnic
description: Electric Picnic is Ireland's largest music and arts festival, held at Stradbally Hall in County Laois. Since 2004, it has grown to 70,000 capacity with a diverse lineup spanning rock, electronic, indie, and comedy, alongside art installations, theatre, and wellness areas in a beautiful Georgian estate setting.

name: Electric Zoo Festival
description: Electric Zoo is a major electronic dance music festival held annually over Labor Day weekend on Randall's Island in New York City. Since 2009, the festival has been one of the East Coast's premier EDM events, showcasing the biggest names in house, techno, trance, dubstep, and drum and bass. The Manhattan skyline provides a stunning backdrop as over 100 artists perform across multiple stages over three days. Electric Zoo is known for its elaborate animal-themed stage designs and immersive production. Past performers include Tiësto, Skrillex, Deadmau5, Martin Garrix, and Diplo.

name: Exit
description: EXIT Festival is one of Southeast Europe's largest music festivals, held at the Petrovaradin Fortress in Novi Sad, Serbia. Founded in 2000 as a student protest for peace and democracy during the political turmoil in Serbia, EXIT has evolved into an award-winning festival drawing 200,000+ visitors over four days. The 18th-century fortress overlooking the Danube provides one of the most dramatic festival settings anywhere in the world. EXIT has won Best Major European Festival at the EU Festival Awards and features a diverse lineup spanning EDM, rock, hip-hop, and reggae.

name: Festival Interceltique de Lorient
description: The Festival Interceltique de Lorient is one of the world's largest Celtic cultural festivals, held annually in Lorient, Brittany, France. Founded in 1970, it celebrates the music, dance, and traditions of Celtic nations and regions — Brittany, Ireland, Scotland, Wales, Cornwall, the Isle of Man, Galicia, and Asturias. Over 10 days in August, the festival attracts 800,000 visitors with hundreds of concerts, parades, and events. Each year the festival honors a specific Celtic nation with a dedicated program. It is both a major music festival and a vital gathering for Celtic cultural identity.

name: Festival Internacional de Benicàssim
description: Festival Internacional de Benicàssim (FIB) is a major music festival held in the coastal town of Benicàssim, Spain, between Valencia and Barcelona. Founded in 1995, FIB runs for four days each July and is known for its eclectic indie, rock, and electronic lineups alongside the Mediterranean beach lifestyle. The festival's daytime schedule is light — with most performances happening from evening into the early morning hours — allowing festivalgoers to spend days at the nearby beach. FIB has hosted Arctic Monkeys, The Killers, Radiohead, Muse, and Massive Attack among many others. Its combination of world-class music and Spanish beach culture has made it a favorite among British and European festivalgoers.

name: Firefly Music Festival
description: Firefly Music Festival takes place in The Woodlands of Dover, Delaware, transforming 105 acres of forest into a four-day music and camping festival. Launched in 2012, Firefly quickly established itself as a major East Coast multi-genre festival with lineups spanning rock, indie, hip-hop, electronic, and pop. The festival's wooded setting provides natural shade and an intimate atmosphere despite its 40,000+ capacity. Past headliners include Paul McCartney, Eminem, The Killers, and Billie Eilish. The Firefly experience blends big-name performances with a welcoming community feel.

name: Fuji Rock Festival
description: Fuji Rock Festival is Japan's largest outdoor music festival and one of the most beautiful festival settings in the world. Held at the Naeba Ski Resort in Yuzawa, Niigata Prefecture, the festival is nestled in forested mountains with stages connected by boardwalks through the woods. Despite its name, Fuji Rock hasn't been held near Mount Fuji since its inaugural year in 1997 (a typhoon forced a venue change). The three-day festival draws around 40,000 attendees and features an international lineup spanning rock, electronic, world, and folk music. Fuji Rock is renowned for its exceptionally clean grounds — Japanese festival culture emphasizes leaving no trace — and its uniquely serene, nature-immersed atmosphere.

name: Glastonbury Festival
description: Glastonbury Festival is the world's largest greenfield music and performing arts festival, held at Worthy Farm in Pilton, Somerset, England. Founded by Michael Eavis in 1970 — inspired after seeing Led Zeppelin perform at the Bath Festival — it has grown from a small gathering of 1,500 people into a five-day cultural institution attracting over 200,000 attendees. Glastonbury is far more than a music festival: it encompasses theatre, circus, comedy, dance, and hundreds of other arts spread across a site that covers over 900 acres. The Pyramid Stage is its iconic centerpiece, hosting headliners from David Bowie and The Rolling Stones to Billie Eilish and Kendrick Lamar. The festival is deeply committed to environmental causes, donating millions to charities including Greenpeace and Oxfam. Glastonbury typically takes a fallow year every five years to allow the land to recover.

name: Glyndebourne Festival Opera
description: Glyndebourne Festival Opera is one of the world's most prestigious opera festivals, held annually at the Glyndebourne country estate near Lewes in East Sussex, England. Founded in 1934 by John Christie and his wife, soprano Audrey Mildmay, the festival is famous for its tradition of long dinner intervals during which audience members picnic in formal evening dress on the estate's manicured lawns — a quintessentially English cultural institution. The purpose-built 1,200-seat theatre is renowned for its intimate acoustics and world-class productions. Glyndebourne mounts six operas each summer season running from May to August, with productions that regularly transfer to major opera houses worldwide.

name: Governors Ball Music Festival
description: Governors Ball Music Festival is New York City's premier outdoor music festival, held in Flushing Meadows–Corona Park in Queens. Launched in 2011, the festival has quickly become one of the most important urban festivals in the U.S., drawing 150,000 attendees over three days with lineups that span rock, hip-hop, pop, and electronic music. Its New York City setting gives it a unique urban energy, and the festival has hosted headliners including Kanye West, Eminem, Florence + The Machine, and Billie Eilish. Gov Ball — as it's commonly known — also features local NYC food vendors and art installations.

name: Hangout Music Festival
description: Hangout Music Festival is a beachfront music festival held on the white sand beaches of Gulf Shores, Alabama. Since 2010, it has offered a unique festival experience where attendees can go from watching headliners on stage to swimming in the Gulf of Mexico within minutes. The three-day festival features rock, indie, hip-hop, and electronic acts across multiple stages built directly on the beach. Past performers include The Weeknd, Red Hot Chili Peppers, The Killers, and Paramore. The beach setting, warm May weather, and relaxed Gulf Coast atmosphere make it one of America's most distinctive festival experiences.

name: Hurricane Festival
description: Hurricane Festival is one of Germany's biggest rock and alternative music festivals, held in Scheeßel, Lower Saxony. It runs simultaneously with Southside Festival — the two share the same lineup with artists alternating between sites. Founded in 1997, Hurricane draws around 80,000 fans over three days for a lineup spanning rock, indie, punk, hip-hop, and electronic music. Past headliners include The Cure, Depeche Mode, Foo Fighters, Arctic Monkeys, and Green Day. The festival is part of the FKP Scorpio group and is a cornerstone of the German summer festival circuit.

name: I Love Techno
description: I Love Techno is one of Europe's most iconic electronic music events, originally launched in Ghent, Belgium in 1995. The festival became legendary for its massive indoor productions, hosting the biggest names in techno, house, and electronic dance music. After years in Ghent's Flanders Expo, the event relocated to Montpellier, France, and has also held editions in other cities. I Love Techno is known for its high-energy atmosphere, world-class DJ lineups, and elaborate visual production. Artists like Carl Cox, The Chemical Brothers, Sven Väth, and Richie Hawtin have headlined the event.

name: Lollapalooza
description: Lollapalooza is a major American music festival that takes place in Grant Park, Chicago, every August. Originally created by Perry Farrell of Jane's Addiction in 1991 as a touring festival, it became a fixed Chicago institution in 2005. The festival features a diverse lineup spanning rock, hip-hop, electronic, pop, and Latin music across eight stages over four days, drawing around 100,000 attendees daily. Set against the stunning backdrop of the Chicago skyline and Lake Michigan, Lollapalooza has become one of the most important festivals in North America. The brand has expanded globally with editions in Brazil, Argentina, Chile, France, Germany, and India, making it one of the most internationally recognized festival names.

name: Lucerne Festival
description: Lucerne Festival is one of the world's leading classical music festivals, held annually in Lucerne, Switzerland. Founded in 1938 with a gala concert conducted by Arturo Toscanini, the festival takes place at the stunning Jean Nouvel-designed KKL Luzern concert hall on the shores of Lake Lucerne, with panoramic views of the Swiss Alps. The main summer festival runs for five weeks each August-September and features the world's top orchestras, conductors, and soloists. The Lucerne Festival Orchestra — an ensemble of the world's finest musicians assembled specifically for the festival — is its flagship ensemble. Past performers include the Berlin Philharmonic, Vienna Philharmonic, Martha Argerich, and Lang Lang.

name: M'era Luna Festival
description: M'era Luna Festival is one of Europe's largest dark music festivals, held annually at the former Hildesheim-Drispenstedt airfield in Germany. Since 2000, it has drawn around 25,000 fans of gothic rock, industrial, EBM, synthpop, darkwave, and medieval music. The festival features two main stages plus a smaller club stage and runs over two days in August. M'era Luna is known for its elaborate costumes and dark aesthetics among attendees, a massive medieval market, and a welcoming community atmosphere. Artists like Rammstein, The Sisters of Mercy, VNV Nation, and Within Temptation have graced its stages.

name: Monterey Jazz Festival
description: The Monterey Jazz Festival is the longest-running jazz festival in the world, held annually since 1958 at the Monterey County Fairgrounds in California. Founded by jazz disc jockey Jimmy Lyons and musician Dave Brubeck, the three-day festival has hosted virtually every major jazz artist of the past seven decades. The intimate, shaded fairground setting on the central California coast creates an atmosphere that jazz fans consider unmatched. Past performers include Louis Armstrong, Dizzy Gillespie, Thelonious Monk, Miles Davis, Billie Holiday, and Herbie Hancock. The festival is also committed to jazz education, running one of the most respected student jazz competitions in the world.

name: Montreal International Jazz Festival
description: The Montreal International Jazz Festival holds the Guinness World Record as the world's largest jazz festival. Held annually over 10 days in the Quartier des Spectacles in downtown Montreal, Canada, it draws over 2 million visitors with a mix of ticketed concerts and hundreds of free outdoor performances. Founded in 1980, the festival showcases jazz in all its forms — from traditional and bebop to fusion, blues, world music, and experimental — alongside pop, R&B, and electronic acts. Past performers include Miles Davis, Ray Charles, B.B. King, Diana Krall, Herbie Hancock, and Aretha Franklin. Around 650 concerts take place across 10 stages, with roughly two-thirds of them free.

name: Montreux Jazz Festival
description: The Montreux Jazz Festival is one of the most prestigious music festivals in the world, held annually on the shores of Lake Geneva in Montreux, Switzerland since 1967. Despite its name, the festival's programming extends far beyond jazz to include rock, pop, blues, electronic, and world music. It has hosted legends from Miles Davis and Nina Simone to Radiohead and Kendrick Lamar.

name: Nature One
description: Nature One is one of Europe's largest electronic music festivals, held every August at the former NATO missile base Raketenbasis Pydna near Kastellaun in the Hunsrück hills. With over 350 DJs spread across more than 20 stages — including massive open-air arenas, the iconic Century Circus, and underground bunker floors built into Cold War infrastructure — it delivers an unmatched atmosphere where techno, house, and trance reverberate through decommissioned launch pads.

name: New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival
description: The New Orleans Jazz & Heritage Festival — known locally as Jazz Fest — is an annual celebration of the music, food, and culture of New Orleans and Louisiana. Held over two weekends at the Fair Grounds Race Course, Jazz Fest showcases a remarkable range of music: jazz, blues, R&B, gospel, Cajun, zydeco, Afro-Caribbean, folk, Latin, rock, and rap. Founded in 1970, the festival is as much about the food — crawfish Monica, cochon de lait po'boys, mango freeze — as the music. With 100,000+ daily attendance, it is one of the largest and most culturally significant festivals in America, deeply intertwined with the identity of New Orleans.

name: Newport Jazz Festival
description: Newport Jazz Festival is one of the most historically significant jazz festivals in the world, held annually at Fort Adams State Park in Newport, Rhode Island. Founded in 1954 by socialite Elaine Lorillard and jazz impresario George Wein, it was the first major outdoor jazz festival in America and helped establish the modern music festival format. The 1958 documentary 'Jazz on a Summer's Day' captured the festival and became one of the most acclaimed music films ever made. Newport has hosted Miles Davis, Duke Ellington, John Coltrane, Ella Fitzgerald, Nina Simone, and countless other legends. Its seaside setting at Fort Adams, overlooking Narragansett Bay, remains one of jazz's most iconic venues.

name: Outside Lands
description: Outside Lands Music and Arts Festival takes place in Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, each August. Launched in 2008, the festival celebrates San Francisco's culture with a focus on local food, wine, beer, comedy, and art alongside a diverse music lineup. The park's lush setting — eucalyptus groves, meadows, and ocean fog — gives the festival a distinct atmosphere unlike the dusty desert or open-field settings of most major festivals. Outside Lands was the first major U.S. music festival to obtain a cannabis sales license, reflecting San Francisco's progressive culture. Past headliners include Radiohead, Metallica, Kendrick Lamar, and Elton John.

name: Outworld Dortmund
description: Outworld Dortmund brings Klangkuenstler's immersive hard techno concept home to Germany at the Westfalenhallen. The event features the signature all-night-long format with 360-degree production, Funktion One sound, and the custom-built "Outworld Reactor" stage.

name: Outworld Bogota
description: Outworld Bogota is the first South American edition of Klangkuenstler's immersive hard techno experience. Held at the Coliseo MedPlus, the event marks Outworld's expansion into Latin America with the signature all-night-long format, 360-degree production, and Funktion One sound.

name: Outworld Madrid
location: 8-hour all-night-long format — one artist, one journey
description: Outworld is an immersive hard techno experience created by German DJ and producer Klangkuenstler. Born from Berlin's underground rave scene, the concept made its Madrid debut in 2024 at IFEMA and has since become one of the most powerful techno events in Spain. The format is an eight-hour all-night-long set — no other artists, no VIP sections, just Klangkuenstler and 15,000+ people in a 360-degree production built around the "Outworld Reactor," a custom structure of rings, lights, lasers, and elevated platforms. Powered by Funktion One sound systems and a staggering lighting rig including 36 Ayrton Cobra lasers and eight 6x4m LED screens, every position in the venue delivers the same intensity. The third Madrid edition (March 2026) sold out 15,500 tickets in under an hour. Outworld is now expanding globally to Amsterdam, Ibiza, Bogota, and beyond.

name: Outworld Amsterdam
description: Outworld Amsterdam debuted during Amsterdam Dance Event 2025 at the iconic Ziggo Dome, bringing Klangkuenstler's immersive hard techno concept to one of Europe's most important electronic music weeks. The event features the signature Outworld format with 360-degree production and Funktion One sound.

name: Outworld Rotterdam
description: Outworld Rotterdam brings Klangkuenstler's immersive hard techno concept to Rotterdam Ahoy, one of the Netherlands' premier concert venues. The event features the signature Outworld format — an extended all-night-long set with 360-degree production, Funktion One sound, and the custom-built "Outworld Reactor" stage design.

name: Outworld Lyon
description: Outworld Lyon brings Klangkuenstler's immersive hard techno concept to the Halle Tony Garnier, France's third-largest concert venue with a standing capacity of nearly 17,000. The event features the long-format performance focused on schranz and industrial techno with 360-degree production.

name: Pohoda
description: Pohoda is Slovakia's largest and most beloved music festival, held annually at Trenčín Airport. Founded in 1997, it has grown from a small local event into an award-winning festival recognized across Europe for its diverse programming, warm community spirit, and exceptional organization. Pohoda — meaning 'comfort' or 'ease' in Slovak — features over 100 acts spanning rock, electronic, world music, jazz, classical, and literature across multiple stages over two days. The festival has won the European Festival Award for Best Medium-Sized Festival and is known for a uniquely relaxed, family-friendly atmosphere. Past performers include Björk, The Prodigy, Kraftwerk, and Patti Smith.

name: Pori Jazz
description: Pori Jazz is one of the oldest and most respected jazz festivals in Europe, held annually in the coastal city of Pori, Finland since 1966. The nine-day festival draws around 150,000 visitors with a program spanning jazz, blues, soul, funk, and world music across multiple venues in the city center and the main outdoor stage at Kirjurinluoto Arena. Pori Jazz has hosted Ray Charles, B.B. King, Miles Davis, Herbie Hancock, and Stevie Wonder. The festival's main stage sits in a beautiful riverside park setting, and the late-night jam sessions in Pori's bars and clubs are legendary among jazz enthusiasts.

name: Primavera Sound
description: Primavera Sound is one of Europe's most critically acclaimed music festivals, held in Barcelona, Spain each June. Founded in 2001, it has built a reputation for eclectic, forward-thinking lineups that balance mainstream headliners with cutting-edge independent artists — a curatorial approach that has earned it near-universal praise from music critics. The festival takes place at the Parc del Fòrum on Barcelona's waterfront, with the Mediterranean Sea as a backdrop. Primavera is known for championing gender parity in its lineups, becoming the first major festival to achieve a 50/50 gender balance. The brand has expanded to Porto (Primavera Sound Porto), Los Angeles, and Buenos Aires.

name: Provinssi
description: Provinssi (formerly Provinssirock) is one of Finland's oldest and largest music festivals, held annually in Seinäjoki in the Ostrobothnia region. Founded in 1979, the three-day festival draws around 30,000 attendees with a broad lineup covering rock, pop, hip-hop, and electronic music. Provinssi is known for its welcoming atmosphere, strong Finnish artist representation, and the unique setting of the Törnävä open-air area beside the Seinäjoki river. It is considered one of Finland's essential summer festival experiences.

name: Pukkelpop
description: Pukkelpop is one of Belgium's largest music festivals, held in Hasselt, Limburg each August. Since 1985, it has grown from a small punk event into a major three-day festival drawing around 66,000 daily attendees. Pukkelpop is known for its adventurous programming that mixes indie rock, electronic, hip-hop, metal, and experimental acts. The festival suffered a tragic incident in 2011 when a severe storm caused a stage collapse, killing five people — an event that profoundly shaped Belgium's approach to festival safety. Pukkelpop has hosted Radiohead, The Chemical Brothers, Gorillaz, Arctic Monkeys, and Arcade Fire among many others.

name: Reading and Leeds Festivals
description: Reading and Leeds Festivals are a pair of annual music festivals held simultaneously on the August bank holiday weekend in Reading and Leeds, England. The Reading Festival dates back to 1961, making it one of the oldest popular music festivals in the world. The twin festivals share the same lineup, with artists alternating between the two sites. Known for their rock and alternative focus, Reading and Leeds have hosted defining performances from Nirvana, Oasis, The Cure, Arctic Monkeys, and many others. The festivals are a rite of passage for British music fans, particularly popular with younger audiences.

Spanning two weekends, it blends iconic international headliners with celebrated Brazilian artists across multiple stages, drawing attendees from over 100 countries with a collective 11.6 million attendees across its history.

name: Rock am Ring
description: Rock am Ring is one of Germany's largest and most storied rock festivals, held annually at the Nürburgring motorsport complex. Together with its twin festival Rock im Park in Nuremberg, it draws a combined audience of over 150,000 fans. Founded in 1985, Rock am Ring has hosted virtually every major rock and metal act of the last four decades, from Metallica and Iron Maiden to Foo Fighters and System of a Down. The Nürburgring setting — a legendary Formula 1 circuit — gives the festival a unique industrial grandeur. It is considered one of the essential European rock festival experiences.

name: Rock the Ocean's Tortuga Music Festival
description: Founded in 2013 by promoter Chris Stacey under the Rock the Ocean banner, Tortuga Music Festival is a three-day, oceanfront country celebration staged directly on the sand at Fort Lauderdale Beach Park in South Florida. Held each April, it's distinguished by its beach-party format — bare feet, swimwear, and live country in the Atlantic breeze — and by its explicit conservation mission. A portion of every ticket supports Rock the Ocean Foundation, which has directed millions to marine research and ocean cleanup nonprofits, and the festival hosts a "Conservation Village" featuring scientists and environmental groups alongside the music. Past headliners have included Kenny Chesney, Luke Bryan, Jason Aldean, Eric Church, Thomas Rhett, and Blake Shelton, with rock and roots acts rounding out the bill. The compact multi-stage footprint, ocean backdrop, and charity angle make it one of the most unusual stops on the country touring circuit.

name: Rock Werchter
description: Rock Werchter is Belgium's premier rock festival, held annually in the fields of Werchter near Leuven. Since 1974 it has grown into one of Europe's most respected festivals, consistently booking the biggest names in rock, pop, and alternative music. The festival has won the International Live Music Conference's Award for Best Festival multiple times — more than any other event. Spread across four days with multiple stages, Rock Werchter is known for its impeccable organization, excellent sound quality, and a loyal audience that returns year after year. Past headliners include Radiohead, Pearl Jam, Foo Fighters, Metallica, The Rolling Stones, and Coldplay.

name: Rolling Loud
location: 65,000 daily capacity at Hard Rock Stadium, Miami
city: 65,000 daily capacity at Hard Rock Stadium
description: Rolling Loud is the world's largest hip-hop festival, founded in Miami in 2015. What started as a single-day event in a downtown Miami park has exploded into a global brand with editions in Los Angeles, New York, Thailand, and Europe. The three-day Miami flagship at Hard Rock Stadium draws 65,000 fans per day with a lineup that reads like a who's-who of rap: Travis Scott, Future, Playboi Carti, Kendrick Lamar, and Kid Cudi have all headlined. Rolling Loud is unapologetically hip-hop — no genre diversification, no legacy rock acts — and that singular focus has made it the defining rap festival of its generation.

name: Rolling Loud India

name: Roskilde Festival
description: Roskilde Festival is Northern Europe's largest music festival and one of the oldest, held annually since 1971 in Roskilde, Denmark. Founded as a non-profit by two high school students and a local promoter, the festival maintains its ethos of community and social responsibility — all profits are donated to humanitarian and cultural charities. Roskilde draws 130,000 attendees across eight days, featuring around 180 acts ranging from rock, hip-hop, and electronic to experimental and world music. The festival is famous for its Orange Stage — one of the largest outdoor stages in Europe — and its vibrant campsite culture where attendees arrive days early to create elaborate themed camps. Roskilde has hosted career-defining performances from acts like Radiohead, Björk, Kanye West, and Kendrick Lamar.

name: Ruisrock
description: Ruisrock is the second-oldest rock festival in Europe, held annually on the island of Ruissalo near Turku, Finland. Founded in 1970 — just one year after Woodstock — Ruisrock has been a cornerstone of Nordic music culture for over 50 years. The festival takes place in a beautiful natural setting of oak groves and beaches on the island, drawing around 35,000 attendees over three days each July. Ruisrock balances Finnish and international acts across rock, pop, electronic, and hip-hop. Past performers include Iggy Pop, The Cure, Gorillaz, and Florence + The Machine.

description:

name: Shaky Knees Music Festival
description: Shaky Knees Music Festival is an annual three-day rock festival held in Central Park, Atlanta, Georgia. Founded in 2013, it has become one of the premier rock-focused festivals in the United States, with a lineup emphasizing indie rock, alternative, and punk alongside classic rock acts. With a capacity of 40,000, it maintains an intimate feel compared to mega-festivals. Past headliners include The Strokes, Foo Fighters, Beck, Tame Impala, and Green Day. Shaky Knees stands out for its dedication to guitar-driven music in an era of genre-blending lineups.

name: Shambhala
location: Regional District of Central Kootenay, Canada
description: Shambhala Music Festival is a unique electronic music festival held on a private ranch in the Kootenay region of British Columbia, Canada. Founded in 1998, it draws around 15,000 attendees to a remote forested setting along the Salmo River. Shambhala is fiercely independent — it has never had a corporate sponsor and operates entirely on ticket sales. The festival is known for its six uniquely designed stages (each with its own artistic identity and sound system), world-class bass music programming, and a strong emphasis on harm reduction, community, and leaving no trace. The sound systems at Shambhala are legendary in the bass music world.

name: Summerfest
description: Summerfest is the world's largest music festival by attendance, held annually at Henry Maier Festival Park along Milwaukee's lakefront. Running for 11 days each summer since 1968, Summerfest hosts over 800 acts on 12 stages, drawing over 800,000 attendees. The festival spans every genre imaginable — rock, pop, country, hip-hop, R&B, electronic, and more — with a mix of arena headliners and emerging artists. The lakefront setting, affordable ticket prices, and sheer breadth of programming make it a uniquely accessible major festival. Past headliners include Paul McCartney, The Rolling Stones, Bruno Mars, and Billie Eilish.

name: Sziget Festival
description: Sziget Festival is one of Europe's largest and most diverse cultural festivals, held on Óbudai-sziget (Old Buda Island) in the Danube River in Budapest, Hungary. Running for a full week each August, Sziget attracts over 100,000 attendees — more than half from outside Hungary — creating one of Europe's most international festival crowds. Beyond its impressive music lineup spanning rock, pop, electronic, hip-hop, and world music, Sziget offers theatre, art installations, circus performances, workshops, film screenings, and a dedicated beach along the Danube. The island setting gives the festival a unique utopian atmosphere, earning it the nickname 'Island of Freedom.' Past headliners include Foo Fighters, Arctic Monkeys, Florence + The Machine, and Ed Sheeran.

name: The Town
description: The Town is a massive music festival created by Rock in Rio organizers, held at the Autódromo de Interlagos in São Paulo, Brazil. Launched in 2023, it is essentially São Paulo's answer to Rock in Rio — sharing the same production values, multiple stages, and massive scale. The festival draws over 500,000 attendees across multiple days with international and Brazilian headliners spanning rock, pop, hip-hop, and electronic music. As one of the newest major festivals in Latin America, The Town has quickly established itself as a flagship event on the global festival calendar.

name: Tomorrowland
description: Tomorrowland is the world's premier electronic dance music festival, held in the town of Boom, Belgium. What began in 2005 as a modest EDM gathering has grown into a global phenomenon, regularly selling out its 400,000 tickets within minutes. The festival is famous for its extraordinary stage designs — each year reveals an entirely new, fantastical main stage built around an overarching theme, from enchanted forests to mythical kingdoms. Spread across multiple stages and vast grounds, Tomorrowland features the biggest names in electronic music across all subgenres: house, techno, trance, drum and bass, and more. The festival also operates Dreamville, one of the world's largest temporary camping cities, creating a fully immersive multi-day experience. Tomorrowland has expanded internationally with editions in Brazil, Winter editions in the French Alps, and most recently Thailand.

name: Tomorrowland Brasil
description: Tomorrowland Brasil is the South American edition of Belgium's world-famous Tomorrowland electronic music festival, held annually at Parque Maeda in Itu, São Paulo state. The three-day event immerses approximately 180,000 attendees in an elaborate themed fantasy world featuring 150+ artists across six elaborately designed stages.

name: Tuska Open Air Metal Festival
description: Tuska Open Air Metal Festival is Finland's largest heavy metal festival, held annually in Helsinki's Suvilahti district. Since 1998, Tuska has been a pilgrimage for metalheads, featuring the heaviest acts from Finland's world-renowned metal scene alongside international headliners. Finland produces more metal bands per capita than any country on Earth, and Tuska is the celebration of that culture. The three-day urban festival draws around 20,000 fans with lineups spanning classic metal, death metal, black metal, thrash, and doom. Past performers include Slayer, Nightwish, Children of Bodom, and Amon Amarth.

name: Ultra Japan
description: Ultra Japan is the Japanese edition of the globally renowned Ultra Music Festival, held annually in September at Tokyo Odaiba Ultra Park on a waterfront peninsula in Tokyo Bay. Launched in 2014, it draws approximately 100,000 fans with its ULTRA MAIN STAGE, RESISTANCE underground stage, and Odaiba's skyline backdrop, making it Japan's most internationally recognized EDM event.

name: Ultra Korea
description: Ultra Korea is Seoul's edition of the Ultra Music Festival, one of Asia's most prominent electronic music events, held annually in September at Oil Tank Culture Park — a striking venue built within repurposed Cold War-era petroleum storage tanks. name: Ultra Music Festival
description: Ultra Music Festival is one of the world's largest and most recognized electronic music festivals, held annually in downtown Miami during Miami Music Week in March. Founded in 1999 on the sands of Miami Beach, Ultra has grown into a three-day spectacle drawing 165,000 fans to Bayfront Park. The festival's massive main stage — one of the most technically impressive in the world — hosts the biggest names in EDM: Tiësto, Martin Garrix, David Guetta, Carl Cox, and many others have delivered career-defining sets here. Ultra is known for world-premiere performances, surprise appearances, and being the unofficial kickoff to the global festival season. The brand operates Ultra Worldwide, with editions in over 20 countries across five continents.

name: Viña del Mar International Song Festival
description: The Viña del Mar International Song Festival is one of the largest and most important music events in Latin America, held annually in the resort city of Viña del Mar, Chile. Founded in 1960 at the Quinta Vergara amphitheatre, the week-long festival combines a song competition with performances by major international and Latin American artists. It is broadcast across Latin America and Spain to audiences of millions. The festival's audience is famously passionate and demanding — performers can receive either a 'Silver Seagull' (Gaviota de Plata) for excellence or be booed off stage. Past performers include Tom Jones, Ricky Martin, Shakira, and Daddy Yankee.

name: Wacken Open Air
description: Wacken Open Air is the world's largest heavy metal festival, held annually in the small village of Wacken in northern Germany. What started in 1990 as a small open-air concert with 800 attendees has become a global pilgrimage for metal fans, regularly drawing 75,000+ visitors from over 80 countries. The festival features around 100 bands across multiple stages over three days, covering every subgenre of metal from classic heavy metal and thrash to black metal, death metal, and folk metal. Wacken's remote rural setting — surrounded by farmland — adds to its mythic status. The festival is known for its legendary mud (the Wacken Mud) when it rains, its bull-horned logo, and the passionate dedication of its attendees, many of whom return year after year.

name: Waterbomb Festival
description: Waterbomb is South Korea's wildly popular summer water-and-music festival, launched in Seoul in 2015 and now a nationwide and international phenomenon. It combines high-energy K-pop, hip-hop, and EDM performances with massive water fights where crowds battle with water cannons. The festival has expanded to Japan, Thailand, Dubai, and beyond.

name: Watershed Music Festival
description: Launched by Live Nation in August 2012 under the direction of country promoter Brian O'Connell, Watershed Music Festival is the Pacific Northwest's premier country gathering and one of the most scenic country events in the United States. It takes over The Gorge Amphitheatre in George, Washington — the cliffside venue famous for its sweeping views of the Columbia River canyon — for three days each August, combining main-stage sets with a massive adjacent campground where boots, flannel, and tailgates fill the high desert. The inaugural 2012 lineup featured Miranda Lambert, Blake Shelton, and Dierks Bentley, and subsequent years have hosted Luke Bryan, Jason Aldean, Kenny Chesney, Eric Church, Brandi Carlile, Hardy, and Old Dominion. The combination of the Gorge's golden-hour sunsets behind the stage and the festival's camping culture has made it a sell-out annual pilgrimage, routinely filling the amphitheater's roughly 27,500-capacity bowl.

name: Wave-Gotik-Treffen
description: Wave-Gotik-Treffen (WGT) is the world's largest gothic music and culture festival, held annually in Leipzig, Germany over Whitsun weekend. Since 1992 it has grown from a small gathering into a massive event drawing over 20,000 attendees from around the world. WGT spans over 100 venues across Leipzig — churches, theaters, clubs, parks, and industrial spaces — creating an extraordinary city-wide celebration of dark culture. The music covers gothic rock, darkwave, EBM, industrial, neofolk, medieval, and beyond. WGT is as much about the spectacle of its attendees — elaborate costumes and striking aesthetics — as the music itself. The Victorian Picnic in Clara Zetkin Park is one of its most iconic traditions.

name: Way Out West
description: Way Out West is an annual music festival held in Slottsskogen park in Gothenburg, Sweden. Since 2007, it has become one of Scandinavia's premier festivals with 30,000 daily visitors enjoying indie, electronic, hip-hop, and pop across multiple stages in a beautiful urban park setting. Known for being entirely vegetarian and carbon-neutral.

name: Wireless Festival
description: Wireless Festival is one of the UK's biggest urban music festivals, held in London each July. Launched in 2005 in Hyde Park and now at Finsbury Park, Wireless is the UK's premier destination for hip-hop, R&B, grime, and electronic music. The festival has hosted major performances from Drake, Stormzy, Jay-Z, Rihanna, Kendrick Lamar, and many others. Its central London location and urban-focused programming set it apart from the UK's rock-dominated festival circuit, drawing a younger, city-based crowd.

name: Øyafestivalen
description: Øyafestivalen (Øya Festival) is Norway's largest music festival, held in the Tøyen district of Oslo each August. Founded in 1999, Øya has built a reputation for expertly curated lineups that blend major international headliners with cutting-edge Nordic acts. The festival takes place in the medieval park beneath the Munch Museum, with the green surroundings creating an intimate urban-park atmosphere. Øya is a leader in sustainability — it was one of the first major festivals to go entirely eco-certified, serving only organic food and operating with minimal environmental impact. Past performers include Radiohead, Björk, Nick Cave, and Kendrick Lamar.
